Guided Component-Based Program Synthesis . In Proceedings of the 32nd ACM/IEEE International Conference on Software Engineering (ICSE) , pp. 215–224, May 2010. Extended tech report version available here . Awarded the ICSE M

report version available here . Awarded the ICSE Most Influential Paper Award . Download [pdf] Abstract We present a novel approach to automatic synthesis of loop-free programs. The approach is based on a combination of oracle-guided lear

on a combination of oracle-guided learning from examples, and constraint-based synthesis from components using satisfiability modulo theories (SMT) solvers. Our approach is suitable for many applications, including as an aid t

table for many applications, including as an aid to program understanding tasks such as deobfuscating malware. We demonstrate the efficiency and effectiveness of our approach by synthesizing bit-manipulating programs and deobfuscating
